info.name: TrustPe API
info.description:
Phase 1 Sprint 1 — auth foundation. Health + auth (signup, login, verify-otp, refresh, logout) + /me.

## Setup

1. Boot the backend: `cd backend && npm run dev`.
2. Update the `baseUrl` collection variable if your backend runs on a non-default port.
3. Run requests in order from top to bottom — `verify-otp` auto-captures the access + refresh tokens into collection variables.
4. Email OTP is logged to your backend terminal in stub mode (no RESEND_API_KEY). Copy the 6-digit code from the log into the `otp` field of the verify-otp request before sending.

## Auth flow

- **Signup** → 202 with `otpSentTo`. Check backend terminal for the OTP.
- **Verify-otp** → 200 with tokens; auto-saves to vars.
- **Get /me** → 200 with your user (uses `{{accessToken}}` automatically).
- **Refresh** → 200, new tokens; auto-saves; old refresh token is now dead.
- **Logout** → 204; refresh token revoked.

Replaying an already-rotated refresh token returns 401 `refresh_chain_killed` (theft detection).

request.description: Liveness probe. Returns `{ ok: true, service, version, sprint, timestamp }`.

item.name: Auth

exec: console.log('Check your backend terminal for the OTP. Then send the verify-otp request with that 6-digit code.');

request.description:
Create a new user OR re-issue an OTP for an existing user with the same email.

Body:
- `phone` — 10-digit Indian mobile (server normalizes to +91XXXXXXXXXX)
- `email` — primary identifier; OTP is sent here
- `name` — display name (letters, spaces, .' - allowed)

Response: 202 with `{ data: { otpSentTo } }`. OTP appears in the backend terminal in stub mode.

exec: console.log('Check your backend terminal for the OTP, then send verify-otp.');

request.description: Issue a fresh OTP for an existing user. If the email doesn't match any user the response is still 202 (no info disclosure).

exec: console.log('Saved accessToken, refreshToken, userId to collection variables.');

request.description:
Verify the 6-digit OTP. On success, returns access + refresh tokens + the user object. The first successful verify moves the user from `unverified` → `onboarding`.

IMPORTANT: replace `123456` with the actual OTP from your backend terminal. The test script auto-saves the returned tokens into `{{accessToken}}` and `{{refreshToken}}`.

Error codes: `otp_not_found`, `otp_incorrect`, `otp_expired`, `otp_too_many_attempts`.

Read the authenticated user's own data. Uses `{{accessToken}}` automatically.

Response includes `id`, `phone`, `email`, `name`, `handle`, `status`, `kycStatus`, `roles`, `trustScore`, `trustBand`.

exec: console.log('Tokens rotated. Old refreshToken is now revoked.');

request.description: Exchange the refresh token for a new pair. Rotation chain + theft detection: the old refresh token is revoked atomically with the new one being issued. Replaying the OLD token after this request returns 401 `refresh_chain_killed` and revokes ALL tokens in the family (force-logout).
Revokes the current refresh token without killing the family. Returns 204. The access token continues to work until it expires (15 min) — for hard logout, clear it client-side too.

item.description: Auth endpoints. Run signup → verify-otp (with the OTP from the backend terminal) → me / refresh / logout.
